title: FAQ 2 - Behind on Payroll Source Deduction Payments
full_text: the letters are going to come in. So if you are behind on payroll, now we talked to a company this morning, really early too. I had to get up at four for this conversation because the guy was in Germany at the time. He has $3 million of payroll that he has not paid to the CRA. And his only solution is to sell the corporation to me and let me take that debt over from him. And then the CRA can sue me, which you know they won't do. So it's possible for anybody to get behind the eight ball when you're dealing with payroll, which is also why I have a payroll company. And I'll cover that last. I'm going to solve everybody's payroll problems in tonight's show. So let's go on to question number two. Am I personally liable for my corporation's payroll debt? Here's the two sub questions. Does the CRA use director's liability provisions to come after me personally? Yes. Yes, they will. Which is why Bugs Bunny, Spider-Man, the Incredible Hulk, Arnold Schwarzenegger, Abraham Lincoln, and well, Mark Carney should all be the directors of your corporations. Now, they say in the law, it's illegal to put down your alias. Yeah. And you know what the punishment is? If you use an alias, they dissolve your corporation. Yeah. You revive it and sell it to me. Stupid. How stupid are these people? Mike, that obviously really they don't have any laws to back them up. So that's the best they can do. We're going to get really mad and nothing happens. All right. So yes, but the director, they will always try to go after the director of a corporation. Please if you are the director of a corporation right now we can change the structure of your company so you are 100 shareholder but you not the director You cannot be the director of your corporation period So if so, what is the process and timeline before they can enforce it? All right. Well, the CRA says they can enforce it any time that they want. They do whatever they want, even though it's against the law what they do. And if you're not going to sue the CRA or file criminal charges against them, obviously they will get away with it. You want to make sure, though, that if you do have payroll debt, just sell it to me. Get you into a brand new corporation. Start over at zero. Don't take these chances. It's a pain in the ass. Yeah, it's a matter of being proactive as opposed to reactive. if you're always reacting to stimulus someone's bothering you and you react someone's taking something from you and then you react well the idea would be to be proactive so if you know who's stealing your stuff I don't know maybe I can't say to do what but you know what I'm getting at make it so that they can't do it anymore all right or if you know that you've got tax trouble just contact me let's get your tax issues resolved before the CRA turns them into real problems for you. Don't let your payroll debt go for years and years and years. Don't let your GST debt go for years and years and years. Actually, you know what's cool, though, guys? It's GST slash HST and payroll. Those are the only two that they will chase you for. They actually won't chase you for corporate income tax debt. Payroll, they absolutely will. The payroll department, they think it's their money. They're crazy. They really do think it's their money.
